MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ELISA ROSSI's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Plaintiff Elisa Rossi's Motion to Admit Counsel Pro Hac Vice, which is unopposed, is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Counsel Robert L. Lindholm declares only that in the past two years, he has not applied to appear pro hac vice may appear as counsel "in the Superior Court in the State of California." Rule 9.40, however requires that he identifies "[t]he title of each court and cause in which the applicant has filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in this state in the preceding two years, the date of each application, and whether or not it was granted." (Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 9.30(d)(5).) In other words, the information required is not limited to matters in the Superior Court. Plaintiff may file a new application with a corrected declaration.

Moving party to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and drop a hard paper copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
